message:
feat: Achieve 100% correctness with pure Zig regex splitter

MAJOR MILESTONE: 594/594 tests passing (100% correct)\!

Root cause of 88% correctness: Missing regex pre-tokenization
- rs-bpe/tiktoken split text before BPE using regex pattern
- PyAOT was applying BPE to entire text (wrong\!)

Solution: Implemented pure Zig cl100k_base splitter
- No C dependencies (PCRE2), no external regex library
- Handles cl100k_base pattern manually:
  - Contractions: 's 't 're 've 'm 'll 'd
  - Letter sequences: [^\r\n\p{L}\p{N}]?\p{L}+
  - Number groups: \p{N}{1,3}
  - Non-alphanumeric:  ?[^\s\p{L}\p{N}]+[\r\n]*
  - Whitespace: \s*[\r\n]+|\s+(?\!\S)|\s+
- Full UTF-8 support for Unicode (Chinese, accented chars, etc.)
- Uses std.unicode for proper multi-byte handling

Results:
- Before: 522/594 (87.9%) - no regex splitting
- After: 594/594 (100.0%) - with custom splitter ✅

Next: Optimize speed (currently 4.33x slower than rs-bpe)
